channel.title: SEA Policy & Capital Watch
channel.description: Policy developments and capital flows shaping Southeast Asian food systems — regulators, development banks, investors, and the architecture around them.

item.title: Monetary Authority of Singapore revises transition finance taxonomy for agriculture
item.description: The Monetary Authority of Singapore has published a revised transition finance taxonomy that for the first time includes a distinct agricultural-systems category. The draft separates productivity-oriented "climate-smart agriculture" from regenerative and agroecological pathways, a taxonomic split that campaigners have pushed for over several years.

item.title: Bank of Thailand consults on green agri-loan framework with cooperative carve-out
item.description: The Bank of Thailand has opened consultation on a green agricultural-lending framework that would assign preferential capital treatment to loans originated to registered farmer cooperatives. The draft references agroecological transition explicitly and treats system finance to smallholder-centred structures as a distinct asset class.

item.title: Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas tightens capital requirements for agri-lending banks
item.description: The Philippine central bank has raised capital adequacy requirements for banks heavily exposed to monoculture agricultural portfolios, citing concentration and climate-transition risk. The rule does not mandate particular farming models but creates a structural incentive for lenders to diversify into smaller-parcel and cooperative-origin loans.

item.title: ADB launches $200 million regenerative agriculture facility for Mekong subregion
item.description: The Asian Development Bank has announced a $200 million facility targeting agroecological transition projects across Vietnam, Cambodia, Laos, and Myanmar. The facility is structured to prioritise cooperative-owned borrowers and concessional blended terms, and represents ADB's first dedicated agroecology vehicle in the region.

item.title: FAIRR Initiative publishes 2026 Coller Protein Producer Index with SEA focus
item.description: The FAIRR Initiative has released the 2026 edition of the Coller Protein Producer Index, adding four Southeast Asian poultry and aquaculture producers to its coverage. Several SEA names sit in the bottom quartile on antibiotic stewardship and feed-systems risk, a result FAIRR argues should prompt investor re-engagement on transition lock-in.

item.title: Aquaspark closes $280 million Fund III with SEA aquaculture mandate
item.description: The Singapore-based impact investor Aquaspark has closed its third aquaculture fund at $280 million, with around 45 per cent of the mandate earmarked for Southeast Asia. Partners describe a deliberate tilt toward smallholder-clustered supply models and away from vertically integrated industrial farms.

item.title: Temasek takes minority stake in regional alternative-protein holding company
item.description: Temasek has taken a minority stake in a Singapore-domiciled holding company consolidating four early-stage alternative-protein businesses across Asia. The investment is being positioned as part of Temasek's climate-smart protein thesis, with portfolio companies ranging from precision fermentation to plant-based seafood.

item.title: ASEAN ministers endorse regional sustainable food systems framework in Jakarta
item.description: ASEAN agriculture ministers meeting in Jakarta have endorsed a regional sustainable food systems framework that names agroecology as one of four transition pathways alongside digitalisation, productivity, and climate resilience. The framework is non-binding but creates shared language for national-level policymaking across the region.

item.title: UOB launches cooperative-tier agricultural lending product for SEA markets
item.description: United Overseas Bank has launched a tiered agricultural lending product specifically underwritten against registered farmer cooperatives rather than individual smallholders or corporate off-takers. The bank says the structure is intended to make system finance available at a level where it has historically been scarce.

item.title: Indonesia launches review of nitrogen fertiliser subsidy programme
item.description: The Indonesian Ministry of Agriculture has opened a formal review of the country's long-standing nitrogen fertiliser subsidy programme, with Ministry of Finance representation on the review panel. Observers describe visible tension between the two ministries over whether subsidy reform should be framed as agroecological transition or as fiscal consolidation.

item.title: DBS Bank signals reduced appetite for industrial livestock financing in SEA
item.description: DBS Bank has told clients it will reduce its lending exposure to large-scale industrial livestock operations across Southeast Asia over the next 36 months, citing physical climate and regulatory-transition risk. The bank has not committed to redirecting freed capacity toward agroecological alternatives, but the announcement is being read by campaigners as an implicit acknowledgement of transition lock-in in the sector.

item.title: Philippines agrarian reform bill gains additional $180 million appropriation
item.description: The Philippine Congress has approved a $180 million supplementary appropriation for the country's agrarian reform programme, with a portion ring-fenced for cooperative capacity-building. The measure sits alongside a broader legislative push to strengthen smallholder equity in export-oriented supply chains.

item.title: AVPN launches Asia Agroecology Funders Collaborative with 14 founding members
item.description: The Asia Venture Philanthropy Network has convened an Asia Agroecology Funders Collaborative with 14 founding members pooling grant and concessional capital. The collaborative's stated goal is to move beyond project-by-project funding and toward system finance for cooperative and smallholder-centred infrastructure across the region.

item.title: Singapore opens $30 million alternative-protein R&D grant round
item.description: Singapore's Enterprise Development agency has opened a $30 million grant round targeting alternative-protein R&D, with emphasis on precision fermentation and cultivated-meat scale-up. The programme frames alternative proteins as central to the country's climate-smart protein strategy and regional export ambitions.

item.title: Malaysia adjusts crude palm oil export levy thresholds
item.description: Malaysia's Plantation Industries Ministry has adjusted the threshold bands for the country's crude palm oil export levy, a routine fiscal measure tied to global price movements. The change is not accompanied by sustainability-linked conditions and has been described by analysts as orthogonal to the ongoing regional agroecology debate.
